title: Typed naming grammar (self-hosting)
description: The controlled vocabularies and composition grammars every named artifact decomposes into. bin/naming.py consumes this data to validate a name of a given class; a name that fails the grammar is a defect caught mechanically. See references/agentic-systems-foundations/naming-conventions.md. The naming system is itself a cell in the ontology — extending a vocab is an ontology revision, never an ad-hoc coinage.
_block_note: The `block` vocabulary is the closed set {lattice, frontier, cell, signal, kit} unioned with every `layer` value; bin/naming.py computes it. (NB: `block`/`unblock` are also OPERATION verbs on a cell's condition flag — distinct axis from the `block` object vocab; both are intentional, 0.4.1 ontology revision.)

plugin.doc: frontier-kernel, frontier-kit-corpus, harness-forge (the `forge`/`factory`/`ops` tiers are the nonoun-plugins marketplace family axis, added as a deliberate ontology revision so a catalog plugin's own name parses its own grammar)

critic_file.doc: critic-partial-order.md, critic-reward-hacking.md — the catalog's reviewer prefix; load-bearing, since check-trust-boundary gates agents/critic-*.md, so a critic named any other way silently escapes the guard gate

rules: Directories mirror enums byte-for-byte (singular): spec/, never specs/.
The namespace never repeats inside members (BEM scoping applied to plugins).
Identity excludes state: cell IDs carry layer.scope.slug; maturity is a Property.
Casing by class: kebab-case plugins/skills/agents; snake_case scripts/tools; PascalCase schema $ids; dot-separated cell-id axes.
Gateverbs carry the control species: gate-* blocks, emit-* injects feedback, propagate-* cascades graph effects.
